action.reasoning: Observation: v27 achieves max streak=60 safe steps in best epi (avg~18), but many eps have 0 safe + extreme collisions (up to 1500) indicating stuck/pushing behavior; detector works but OA treats human as obstacle or fails to escape collisions; never reaches 500 safe. Earlier versions hit ~50-100 safe. Config exact (500 steps in [1-2)m at |angle|<0.2618). Analysis: OA gating insufficient (human in depth when detector high causes wrong turns/stuck); speed-match/angle control drifts on dynamic oracle; high-coll eps dominate avg. Root cause is OA interfering near human + insufficient recovery from collisions. Literature heuristic can reach high follow-rate with proper OA and stabilization. Decision: rewrite COMPLETE non-truncated test_full_episode.py + predict.py + train.py with v28 HeuristicPolicy (strict OA only if detector<0.2+min_front<0.8, turn-to-open-side with correct sign based on left_d vs right_d, strong backup on collision-like conditions, follow mode with base=0.25+0.2*err clipped, pure-rotate if |angle|>0.4 with linear=0 + high gain, gentler control when detector high+safe_range, refined depth slices (center 30% for min_front, sides 25%), per-step safe/streak/max_streak/coll/detector tracking, improved collision_delta that handles all dict cases, full JSON contract with make_serializable+all required keys+manual safe>=500 for nav_seek_success+metrics fallback+per_episode aggregates as means). Update dummy ckpt to v28. Run test_full_episode.py (5 eps) expecting reduced collisions + higher max_streak/safe (>100 target) before final submit_eval.
